ddls:
CREATE TABLE "network_events" (
  "event_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each network event */,
  "event_time" TEXT /* Timestamp of the network event */,
  "plat_id" INTEGER /* Identifier for the platform used */,
  "area_id" INTEGER /* Identifier for the area where the event occurred */,
  "world_id" INTEGER /* Identifier for the world associated with the event */,
  "user_id" TEXT /* Unique identifier for the user */,
  "role_channel" INTEGER /* Role channel identifier indicating user type */,
  "network_type" TEXT /* Type of network used (e.g., WIFI, 4G) */,
  "create_hour" INTEGER /* Hour of creation for the event log */,
  "event_type" TEXT /* Type of event (e.g., login, logout, data transfer) */,
  "data_volume" TEXT /* Volume of data transferred during the event */,
  "connection_duration" TEXT /* Duration of the user's connection */,
  "event_status" TEXT /* Status of the event (e.g., successful, failed, pending) */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("event_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_network_events_network_type FOREIGN KEY ("network_type") REFERENCES network_types ("type_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_network_events_plat_id FOREIGN KEY ("plat_id") REFERENCES platforms ("plat_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_network_events_role_channel FOREIGN KEY ("role_channel") REFERENCES role_channels ("role_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_network_events_user_id FOREIGN KEY ("user_id") REFERENCES users ("user_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_network_events_world_id FOREIGN KEY ("world_id") REFERENCES worlds ("world_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_network_events_area_id FOREIGN KEY ("area_id") REFERENCES areas ("area_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "platforms" (
  "plat_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each platform */,
  "plat_name" TEXT /* Name of the platform */,
  "description" TEXT /* Description of the platform */,
  "platform_version" TEXT /* Version of the platform */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("plat_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "areas" (
  "area_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each area */,
  "area_name" TEXT /* Name of the area */,
  "region" TEXT /* Region to which the area belongs */,
  "country" TEXT /* Country where the area is located */,
  "city" TEXT /* City within the area */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("area_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "worlds" (
  "world_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each world */,
  "world_name" TEXT /* Name of the world */,
  "environment" TEXT /* Environment (e.g., production, testing) */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("world_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "users" (
  "user_id" TEXT /* Unique identifier for each user */,
  "user_name" TEXT /* Full name of the user */,
  "role" TEXT /* Role of the user in the organization */,
  "email" TEXT /* Email address of the user */,
  "phone_number" TEXT /* Phone number of the user */,
  "address" TEXT /* Address of the user */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("user_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "role_channels" (
  "role_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for the role channel */,
  "role_name" TEXT /* Name of the role channel */,
  "description" TEXT /* Description of the role channel */,
  "permissions" TEXT /* Permissions associated with the role channel */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("role_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "network_types" (
  "type_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each network type */,
  "type_name" TEXT /* Name of the network type (e.g., WIFI, 4G) */,
  "description" TEXT /* Description of the network type */,
  "bandwidth" TEXT /* Bandwidth of the network type */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("type_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "devices" (
  "device_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each device */,
  "device_name" TEXT /* Name of the device */,
  "device_type" TEXT /* Type of device (e.g., laptop, smartphone) */,
  "operating_system" TEXT /* Operating system of the device */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("device_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "device_events" (
  "event_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each device event */,
  "device_id" INTEGER /* Identifier for the device */,
  "event_time" TEXT /* Timestamp of the device event */,
  "event_type" TEXT /* Type of device event (e.g., login, logout) */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("event_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_device_events_device_id FOREIGN KEY ("device_id") REFERENCES devices ("device_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "network_performance" (
  "metric_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each network performance metric */,
  "event_id" INTEGER /* Identifier for the network event */,
  "metric_name" TEXT /* Name of the performance metric (e.g., latency, packet loss) */,
  "metric_value" TEXT /* Value of the performance metric */,
  "timestamp" TEXT /* Timestamp of the performance metric */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("metric_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_network_performance_event_id FOREIGN KEY ("event_id") REFERENCES network_events ("event_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "user_sessions" (
  "session_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each user session */,
  "user_id" TEXT /* Identifier for the user */,
  "start_time" TEXT /* Start time of the user session */,
  "end_time" TEXT /* End time of the user session */,
  "duration" TEXT /* Duration of the user session */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("session_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_user_sessions_user_id FOREIGN KEY ("user_id") REFERENCES users ("user_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "dashboards" (
  "dashboard_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each dashboard */,
  "department" TEXT /* Department associated with the dashboard (e.g., marketing, customer service) */,
  "dashboard_name" TEXT /* Name of the dashboard */,
  "description" TEXT /* Description of the dashboard */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("dashboard_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "widgets" (
  "widget_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each widget */,
  "dashboard_id" INTEGER /* Identifier for the dashboard */,
  "widget_name" TEXT /* Name of the widget */,
  "description" TEXT /* Description of the widget */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("widget_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_widgets_dashboard_id FOREIGN KEY ("dashboard_id") REFERENCES dashboards ("dashboard_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "reports" (
  "report_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each report */,
  "report_name" TEXT /* Name of the report */,
  "description" TEXT /* Description of the report */,
  "report_date" TEXT /* Date the report was generated */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("report_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "report_metrics" (
  "metric_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each report metric */,
  "report_id" INTEGER /* Identifier for the report */,
  "metric_name" TEXT /* Name of the metric (e.g., network usage, latency) */,
  "metric_value" TEXT /* Value of the metric */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("metric_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_report_metrics_report_id FOREIGN KEY ("report_id") REFERENCES reports ("report_id")
)
